name: install
context: Install, add, update, remove, fetch, and inspect pnpm dependencies with lockfile-aware workspace scoping
info: This skill covers the dependency lifecycle for pnpm projects: reproducible installs with `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, workspace-aware `pnpm add` and `pnpm remove`, recursive updates, `pnpm outdated`, and `pnpm fetch` for Docker or offline workflows. It matters because pnpm's workspace filters, build-script approvals, and store-backed fetch behavior are materially different from npm and affect how an autonomous agent should mutate or hydrate a repository.
triggers: add a dependency in a pnpm project
run a frozen pnpm install in CI
body:
# pnpm Install and Dependency Mutation

Use pnpm's install surface when the dependency graph itself must change or be materialized. The safest default in automation is a lockfile-driven install; the safest default in monorepos is a filtered install or add.

```bash
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm add -D typescript vitest
pnpm add react --filter @acme/web
pnpm update -r
pnpm outdated -r
pnpm fetch --prod
```

Because pnpm reuses a global store, `fetch` can pre-populate artifacts from `pnpm-lock.yaml` without writing project `node_modules`. That pattern is especially useful before an offline install or Docker build. For build-script approval, combine this node with [[pnpm/security]].

sections.title: Reproducible installs
sections.body: Use `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` in CI or any automation that should not rewrite the dependency graph. This fails if `package.json` and `pnpm-lock.yaml` disagree. The installation docs and CI examples consistently pair lockfile installs with a pinned pnpm version from Corepack or another official installer. For package-manager pinning, load [[pnpm/config#package-manager-pin]].

sections.title: Add, remove, and update in a workspace
sections.body: Use `--filter <selector>` to change one package or part of the graph instead of mutating the entire workspace. `pnpm add --workspace` only succeeds when the dependency exists locally in the workspace, and `-w` or `--workspace-root` explicitly targets the repo root package. This is the correct default when a monorepo root contains tooling but applications live below it. See [[pnpm/workspace#filters]].

sections.title: Fetch and offline workflows
sections.body: The official `pnpm fetch` docs emphasize that it only needs `pnpm-lock.yaml` and fills the store without creating `node_modules`. A common pipeline is `pnpm fetch`, then `pnpm install --offline --frozen-lockfile`. This is ideal for Docker layers or CI runners where network access should happen once and installs should later reuse the cached store. See [[pnpm/store#caching-in-ci]] and [[pnpm/deploy#docker-and-ci]].

sections.title: Updating and inspecting stale dependencies
sections.body: Use `pnpm update` to refresh dependencies, optionally with `-r` for recursive workspaces, and `pnpm outdated` to inspect drift before mutating manifests. When a package should not be updated automatically, prefer config-based ignore lists or catalogs instead of ad hoc shell logic. Load [[pnpm/config#catalogs-and-overrides]] when a workspace wants a single declared version source.

scripts.name: Manage Dependencies
scripts.description: Run pnpm install, add, remove, update, fetch, or outdated with workspace-aware flags

CI.description: Lets pnpm know it is running in CI when not auto-detected

PNPM_HOME.description: Optional pnpm home directory if the binary is not on the default PATH

NPM_CONFIG_REGISTRY.description: Override the package registry for install or fetch operations

packages.description: Package specs for add, remove, or targeted update

filters.description: pnpm filter selectors used to scope the command

workspaceRoot.description: Target the workspace root with -w when supported

offline.description: Require the local pnpm store with no network access

frozenLockfile.description: Fail instead of changing the lockfile
